review_context.purpose: Claim-level evidence registry for future local annotations on the AI agents taxonomy page.
review_context.important_rule: A source ID in this registry means a candidate evidence object exists. A claim is supported only when the source appears in supporting_source_ids and source_support explains what the source actually supports.

source_status_values.internal-negative-finding: Internal research note that limits or contests a claim.
source_status_values.internal-audit: Internal audit or workflow evidence; useful for rewrite process, not public taxonomy support.
source_status_values.source-stub: Mentioned in the current page/footer or audit but not yet cataloged in research/sources.md; must be reviewed before carrying a public claim.

anthropic-building-effective-agents.title: Building Effective Agents
anthropic-building-effective-agents.authors_or_org: Erik Schluntz and Barry Zhang, Anthropic
anthropic-building-effective-agents.supports: Workflow-vs-agent distinction, augmented LLM practitioner vocabulary, workflow patterns, start-simple guidance.
anthropic-building-effective-agents.does_not_support: A field-wide consensus taxonomy or universal claim that every agent system is built from Anthropic's exact primitive.

anthropic-writing-tools-for-agents.title: Writing Tools for Agents
anthropic-writing-tools-for-agents.source_type: official engineering blog / tool design case study
anthropic-writing-tools-for-agents.supports: Tool names, descriptions, schemas, outputs, and error handling as part of the agent reasoning and safety surface.
anthropic-writing-tools-for-agents.does_not_support: A formal theorem that agent tools are not software APIs, or a universal safety model for every tool runtime.

anthropic-multi-agent-research-system.title: How We Built Our Multi-Agent Research System
anthropic-multi-agent-research-system.source_type: official engineering blog / production case study
anthropic-multi-agent-research-system.supports: Supervisor-worker research topology, parallel subagents, measured cost/trace trade-offs in broad research tasks.
anthropic-multi-agent-research-system.does_not_support: A general claim that multi-agent architectures outperform simpler workflows across domains.

openai-practical-guide-building-agents.title: A Practical Guide to Building Agents
openai-practical-guide-building-agents.supports: Incremental agent design, orchestration, tools, guardrails, and evaluation-oriented deployment.
openai-practical-guide-building-agents.does_not_support: A complete ontology or proof that one architecture is best for all tasks.

openai-agents-sdk-docs.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Documentation
openai-agents-sdk-docs.supports: Current OpenAI vocabulary for agents, hosted tools, handoffs, and guardrails.
openai-agents-sdk-docs.does_not_support: Hidden internals of OpenAI products or durable claims about future SDK features.

openai-agents-sdk-tracing.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Tracing Documentation
openai-agents-sdk-tracing.supports: Agent traces, spans, model calls, tool calls, and handoffs as observable execution units.
openai-agents-sdk-tracing.does_not_support: A claim that tracing alone selects or validates an architecture.

openai-computer-using-agent.title: OpenAI Computer Use Tool Documentation
openai-computer-using-agent.supports: Computer-use loops involving screenshots, actions, and interaction with external interfaces.
openai-computer-using-agent.does_not_support: Broad product claims about all browser operators or hidden product topology.

openai-operator-system-card.title: Operator System Card
openai-operator-system-card.supports: Browser-agent safety framing, confirmations, restricted actions, monitoring, and adversarial risks.
openai-operator-system-card.does_not_support: A claim that human approval alone is sufficient for safe agent operation.

mcp-spec-2025-11-25.title: Model Context Protocol Specification
mcp-spec-2025-11-25.supports: MCP primitives and scope for connecting AI applications to tools, prompts, resources, context, and transports.
mcp-spec-2025-11-25.does_not_support: Comparative public adoption superiority over A2A.

google-a2a-announcement.title: Announcing the Agent2Agent Protocol
google-a2a-announcement.supports: A2A launch intent and agent-to-agent interoperability scope.
google-a2a-announcement.does_not_support: Current 2026 A2A project status without pairing with the latest spec and Linux Foundation update.

a2a-protocol-spec.title: A2A Protocol Specification
a2a-protocol-spec.supports: A2A concepts such as agent cards, tasks, messages, artifacts, streaming, push notifications, and security.
a2a-protocol-spec.does_not_support: A neutral comparison of A2A adoption against MCP adoption.

linux-foundation-a2a-v1.title: A2A Protocol Surpasses 150 Organizations, Lands in Major Cloud Platforms, and Sees Enterprise Production Use in First Year
linux-foundation-a2a-v1.source_type: official press release / governance and adoption update
linux-foundation-a2a-v1.reliability: primary for project status; weak for comparative adoption
linux-foundation-a2a-v1.supports: A2A v1.0.0, Linux Foundation project activity, and official project adoption claims as of 2026-04-09.
linux-foundation-a2a-v1.does_not_support: An independent adoption study or MCP-vs-A2A market ranking.

langchain-agent-protocol.title: LangChain Agent Protocol
langchain-agent-protocol.supports: Runtime-control protocol scope for running agents.
langchain-agent-protocol.does_not_support: Agent-to-agent interoperability or tool/context exchange as its whole scope.

agntcy-docs.title: AGNTCY Documentation
agntcy-docs.supports: Identity, directory, discovery, and interoperability concerns in agent ecosystems.

opentelemetry-genai-semconv.title: OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions
opentelemetry-genai-semconv.supports: Vendor-neutral vocabulary for tracing GenAI operations.
opentelemetry-genai-semconv.does_not_support: Product-specific evaluation thresholds or architecture choice by itself.

react.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
react.supports: Interleaving reasoning and external actions in language-model agents.
react.does_not_support: All modern agent architectures or all tool-use patterns.

reflexion.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
reflexion.supports: Evaluator/self-feedback loops and memory-like stored lessons.

generative-agents.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
generative-agents.supports: Memory stream, reflection, planning, and social interaction architecture.
generative-agents.does_not_support: Strong emergence as a primary engineering explanation.

memgpt.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
memgpt.supports: Managed memory hierarchy and explicit movement between context and external storage.
memgpt.does_not_support: Memory as merely a bigger context window, or a fixed field-wide four-type taxonomy.

autogen.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
autogen.authors_or_org: Qingyun Wu et al., Microsoft Research
autogen.source_type: paper / preprint; official Microsoft project source
autogen.supports: Conversation-based multi-agent orchestration and role coordination.
autogen.does_not_support: Current product feature claims without newer docs.

magentic-one.title: Magentic-One: A Generalist Multi-Agent System
magentic-one.supports: Orchestrator plus specialized agents for browsing, coding, file handling, and terminal work.
magentic-one.does_not_support: Proof that this topology is generally preferable.

agentbench.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
agentbench.supports: Interactive-environment benchmark design and task-success evaluation.

webarena.title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
webarena.supports: Realistic web-agent task design with actions, state, and success criteria.

swe-bench.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
swe-bench.supports: Coding-agent benchmark design based on real GitHub issues and tests.

gaia.title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
gaia.supports: Benchmark design for mixed reasoning, tools, web browsing, and assistant tasks.

tau-bench.title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
tau-bench.supports: Tool-agent-user interactions under policies and state.
tau-bench.does_not_support: All HITL safety claims or production deployment safety.

osworld.title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
osworld.supports: Desktop/computer-use benchmark design for GUI-level action.

openai-paperbench.title: PaperBench
openai-paperbench.does_not_support: General claims that long-horizon agent tasks are solved.

openai-browsecomp.title: BrowseComp
openai-browsecomp.supports: Hard web-browsing benchmark design for search, source selection, and multi-step synthesis.

agentdojo.title: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
agentdojo.supports: Prompt-injection and tool-use attack evaluation in agentic settings.
agentdojo.does_not_support: A complete production security control framework.

owasp-llm-top-10-2025.title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025
owasp-llm-top-10-2025.supports: Security vocabulary for prompt injection, excessive agency, data leakage, supply-chain risk, and similar risks.
owasp-llm-top-10-2025.does_not_support: Agent-specific protocol security as a complete model.

owasp-agentic-skills-top-10.title: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10
owasp-agentic-skills-top-10.supports: Agentic skill/tool execution risk, authority, manifests, audit logging, and autonomous workflow behavior.
owasp-agentic-skills-top-10.does_not_support: Full product-specific security posture for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or similar tools.

five-eyes-agentic-ai-guidance.title: Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services
five-eyes-agentic-ai-guidance.authors_or_org: Five Eyes cybersecurity agencies, published by New Zealand NCSC
five-eyes-agentic-ai-guidance.supports: Governance, permissions, monitoring, procurement, and controls for agentic AI services.
five-eyes-agentic-ai-guidance.does_not_support: Technical details for any single vendor implementation.

wooldridge-jennings-1995.title: Intelligent Agents: Theory and Practice
wooldridge-jennings-1995.authors_or_org: Michael Wooldridge and Nicholas R. Jennings
wooldridge-jennings-1995.supports: Classic agent properties such as autonomy, social ability, reactivity, and proactiveness.

shoham-1993.title: Agent-Oriented Programming
shoham-1993.does_not_support: A direct mapping from symbolic agents to LLM harnesses.

contract-net-protocol.title: The Contract Net Protocol: High-Level Communication and Control in a Distributed Problem Solver
contract-net-protocol.supports: Task announcement, bidding, award, and market-style multi-agent coordination.
contract-net-protocol.does_not_support: A claim that all modern delegation systems implement Contract Net.

bdi-agents-rao-georgeff.title: BDI Agents: From Theory to Practice
bdi-agents-rao-georgeff.authors_or_org: Anand S. Rao and Michael P. Georgeff
bdi-agents-rao-georgeff.supports: Beliefs, desires, intentions, commitments, and plan selection as classic agent architecture concepts.

cursor-docs-agent-tools-background-agent.title: Cursor Docs: Agent tools; Background Agents
cursor-docs-agent-tools-background-agent.supports: Candidate source for Cursor visible feature claims.
cursor-docs-agent-tools-background-agent.does_not_support: Cursor internal topology until docs are reviewed and specific pages are cataloged.

devin-docs-introducing-scheduled-session.title: Devin Docs: Introducing Devin; Scheduled Sessions
devin-docs-introducing-scheduled-session.supports: Candidate source for Devin visible feature and scheduled-session claims.
devin-docs-introducing-scheduled-session.does_not_support: Hidden Devin architecture or coordination internals until docs are reviewed.

anthropic-claude-code-docs.title: Anthropic Docs: Claude Code overview; Security; Settings
anthropic-claude-code-docs.supports: Candidate source for Claude Code visible tool, terminal, permission, hook, and subagent claims.
anthropic-claude-code-docs.does_not_support: Claude Code security posture as sufficient without independent safety sources.

manus-docs-browser-wide-research.title: Manus Docs: Browser Operator; Wide Research; Zapier integration
manus-docs-browser-wide-research.supports: Candidate source for Manus visible browser/operator and wide research feature claims.
manus-docs-browser-wide-research.does_not_support: Manus internal orchestrator or topology unless official docs expose it.

openai-deep-research-docs.title: OpenAI Help/API Docs: Deep research in ChatGPT; Deep research guide
openai-deep-research-docs.supports: Candidate source for OpenAI deep research feature and supported-tool claims.
openai-deep-research-docs.does_not_support: Absolute read-only boundaries or hidden architecture without exact docs.

autogpt-readme-docs.title: AutoGPT README and docs: Platform blocks; Agent blocks
autogpt-readme-docs.supports: Candidate source for current AutoGPT platform/workflow positioning.
autogpt-readme-docs.does_not_support: A general historical conclusion without dated version context.

openai-function-calling-2023.title: Function calling and other API updates
openai-function-calling-2023.supports: Candidate source for the 2023 function/tool calling timeline milestone.
openai-function-calling-2023.does_not_support: Current function/tool calling API behavior without living docs.

negative-anthropic-pattern-consensus.title: Negative Finding: No peer-reviewed consensus that Anthropic's five patterns are the canonical agent taxonomy
negative-anthropic-pattern-consensus.supports: Caution against consensus/canonical language around Anthropic workflow patterns.

negative-mcp-a2a-adoption.title: Negative Finding: No robust public evidence that MCP has clearly stronger developer adoption than A2A
negative-mcp-a2a-adoption.supports: Caution against MCP-vs-A2A comparative adoption claims.

negative-product-architecture.title: Negative Finding: Product architecture claims are mostly inference
negative-product-architecture.supports: Caution that product docs support visible behavior more than hidden topology.

negative-memory-taxonomy.title: Negative Finding: No universal agent-memory taxonomy
negative-memory-taxonomy.supports: Caution that memory terminology is inconsistent across papers and products.

negative-hitl-sufficiency.title: Negative Finding: HITL is not proven as a sufficient safety control
negative-hitl-sufficiency.supports: Caution that HITL must be paired with permissions, trusted context, monitoring, and audit.

negative-benchmark-leaderboards.title: Negative Finding: Benchmark leaderboards are too volatile for durable page claims
negative-benchmark-leaderboards.supports: Caution to cite benchmark design instead of transient scores.

negative-emergence.title: Negative Finding: Emergence is not a useful primary engineering explanation
negative-emergence.supports: Caution to prefer mechanism-first explanations over emergence claims.
negative-emergence.does_not_support: A philosophical claim about strong emergence.

audit-annotation-ui-spec.title: Evidence Annotation UI Spec
audit-annotation-ui-spec.supports: Annotation fields, marker behavior, and claim/source registry mechanics.

research-sources-catalog.title: Sources Catalog
research-sources-catalog.supports: Existence and review notes for cataloged source records.
research-sources-catalog.does_not_support: The underlying external claims without using the actual external source.

tasks-readme.title: Task Queue README
tasks-readme.supports: Internal rewrite history and process notes.

deferred_priority_claim_ids_due_to_30_cl.reason: Covered partly by C12 and future core taxonomy rewrite.
Covered partly by C32/C33/C35 protocol adoption caveats.
Covered partly by C42/C44 memory boundary entries.
Lower priority than C46 for the decision-guide rewrite; should be added in prompt 12 if needed.

C09.current_claim_summary: Workflow vs Agent is a central axis of consensus.
C09.reasoning_bridge: Anthropic and OpenAI support workflow-vs-agent as a useful practical design distinction, but the research backlog found no peer-reviewed consensus that it is a universal taxonomy axis.

source_support.anthropic-building-effective-agents: Directly supports workflow vs agent practitioner framing.
source_support.openai-practical-guide-building-agents: Supports incremental agent design and orchestration framing.
source_support.negative-anthropic-pattern-consensus: Limits wording from consensus/canonical to influential practical framing.

C09.caveat: Rewrite as a practical axis for this page, not as field-wide consensus.

C10.current_claim_summary: Augmented LLM is the canonical primitive and atom of all modern agent systems.
C10.reasoning_bridge: Anthropic supports augmented LLM as a useful base building block, while OpenAI and ReAct support model-plus-tools patterns, but none of these sources make it a universal atom.

source_support.mcp-spec-2025-11-25: Supports protocolized tool/context access, not the primitive itself.

C10.caveat: Use 'Anthropic's useful base primitive for this map' rather than 'canonical atom of all systems.'

C11.current_claim_summary: The protocol layer is separate and includes MCP, A2A, LangChain Agent Protocol, and AGNTCY.
C11.reasoning_bridge: The specifications and project docs support these as protocol or protocol-ecosystem efforts, but they standardize different surfaces rather than one comparable layer.
C11.caveat: Compare by standardization target, not by a single maturity or adoption ladder.

C12.current_claim_summary: Any agentic system can be described by coordinates; A2 x A3 x A4 is the most independent core.
C12.reasoning_bridge: Practitioner and classic MAS sources support autonomy, topology, and coordination as recurring design dimensions, but 'any system' should be framed as this page's practical map rather than a formal ontology.

source_support.contract-net-protocol: Supports coordination mechanisms as a separate concern.
source_support.autogen: Supports multi-agent topology and role coordination examples.

C12.caveat: Avoid universal wording; say the map is useful for comparing public systems and design choices.

C14.current_claim_summary: A7 includes emergence, latency, cost, observability; token cost is multi-agent x10-15.
C14.reasoning_bridge: Anthropic's multi-agent research case directly supports a large cost increase in one broad research setting, while tracing sources support observability burden, but the multiplier should not be generalized.

source_support.anthropic-multi-agent-research-system: Supports substantial token/cost increase in a specific research system.
source_support.openai-agents-sdk-tracing: Supports trace visibility for agent steps.

C14.caveat: State cost amplification as a design risk to measure per domain, not as a universal multiplier.

C15.current_claim_summary: Every cell in the topology x coordination grid is a real architecture.
C15.reasoning_bridge: Classic and modern sources support multiple topology/coordination combinations, but not every grid cell is meaningful or evidenced.

source_support.anthropic-multi-agent-research-system: Supports supervisor-worker tree-like coordination in one production case.

C15.caveat: Use the grid as a design space with example-backed cells; mark speculative or incoherent combinations.

C16.current_claim_summary: 80% of production systems today live in DAG plus command hierarchy.
C16.reasoning_bridge: No reviewed research source provides a public production adoption dataset or survey for this percentage.
C16.caveat: Delete unless a robust adoption survey or dataset is added; do not replace with another prevalence claim without evidence.

C18.current_claim_summary: Real systems are plotted based on public behavior, not internal architecture.
C18.reasoning_bridge: Official product docs can support visible feature surfaces, while the local negative finding correctly limits inference about hidden topology.

source_support.anthropic-multi-agent-research-system: Provides one unusually detailed architecture case.
source_support.negative-product-architecture: Limits product placement to public control surfaces and documented behavior.

C18.caveat: Every product placement needs access dates and a visible distinction between documented behavior and taxonomy inference.

C19.current_claim_summary: Cursor coordinates and behavior include remote VM, branch, follow-up/takeover, and MCP.
C19.reasoning_bridge: The current page cites Cursor docs only as a footer source, so the registry can only treat Cursor claims as candidates until exact docs are cataloged.

source_support.mcp-spec-2025-11-25: Supports MCP scope only, not Cursor feature claims.
source_support.negative-product-architecture: Warns that visible behavior does not prove internal topology.

C19.caveat: Before publishing, add exact Cursor URLs, access date, and separate documented features from A2/A3/A4 inference.

C20.current_claim_summary: Devin writes, runs, and tests code; works on tickets, bugs, refactors, docs, and recurring sessions.
C20.reasoning_bridge: The footer mentions Devin docs, but no cataloged source currently verifies the exact feature list or coordinate inference.

source_support.swe-bench: Supports coding-agent evaluation task design, not Devin product behavior.
source_support.negative-product-architecture: Limits claims about hidden product architecture.

C20.caveat: Treat feature claims as time-sensitive and coordinate placement as inference until exact Devin docs are reviewed.

C21.current_claim_summary: Claude Code is a terminal harness with file tools, Bash, web/MCP, hooks, subagents, and explicit approval.
C21.reasoning_bridge: Claude Code docs are only a source stub here, while OWASP and Anthropic tool guidance can support risk framing around tool authority and approval.

source_support.anthropic-writing-tools-for-agents: Supports why tool interfaces are part of agent reasoning.
source_support.owasp-agentic-skills-top-10: Supports agentic skill/tool execution risk framing.
source_support.anthropic-claude-code-docs: Candidate source for product feature details; not yet cataloged.

C21.caveat: Do not treat approval as sufficient safety; verify living Claude Code docs before marking product feature claims strong.

C22.current_claim_summary: Manus Browser Operator and Wide Research placement; internal orchestrator undisclosed.
C22.reasoning_bridge: The current caveat is appropriate, but Manus docs are only a source stub and cannot yet support more than a tentative placement.

source_support.webarena: Supports web-agent evaluation dimensions, not Manus product claims.
source_support.openai-browsecomp: Supports browsing/research benchmark dimensions, not Manus product claims.

C22.caveat: Keep 'tentative' unless exact official Manus docs are cataloged and dated.

C23.current_claim_summary: OpenAI deep research is a bounded read-only research agent with MCP search/fetch and code interpreter.
C23.reasoning_bridge: OpenAI agent docs and browsing benchmarks support research-agent evaluation context, but the exact deep research tool boundary needs a cataloged product/API doc.

source_support.openai-deep-research-docs: Candidate source for feature/tool boundaries; not yet cataloged.

C23.caveat: Avoid absolute 'read-only' wording until exact current docs are cited with access date.

C24.current_claim_summary: AutoGPT is now a workflow/hybrid platform contrast.
C24.reasoning_bridge: The footer points to AutoGPT README/docs, but the research catalog has not reviewed them, so the historical contrast must stay dated and provisional.

source_support.negative-product-architecture: Limits platform placement to public docs and visible control surfaces.

C24.caveat: Add exact repo/doc URLs, version or access date, and avoid broad claims about AutoGPT's evolution without dated evidence.

C32.current_claim_summary: MCP is a de-facto standard and A2A is enterprise-niche.
C32.reasoning_bridge: MCP and A2A specs support different technical scopes, and the Linux Foundation update supports A2A activity, but public comparative adoption evidence is explicitly weak.

source_support.linux-foundation-a2a-v1: Supports A2A 2026 project status, not neutral comparative adoption.

C32.caveat: Replace with date-stamped scope wording and avoid 'de-facto standard' unless narrowly defined.

C33.current_claim_summary: A2A released in April 2025, transferred to LF, has a live spec by April 2026, but adoption is enterprise-niche while MCP is more visible.
C33.reasoning_bridge: Launch, spec, and Linux Foundation sources support A2A chronology and project activity, but the adoption comparison remains proxy-based and contested.

source_support.google-a2a-announcement: Supports April 2025 launch and intended scope.
source_support.a2a-protocol-spec: Supports current specification concepts and v1.0.0 status.

C33.caveat: Date the status as reviewed 2026-06-03 and do not infer an adoption ranking.

C35.current_claim_summary: Figure 5 frames MCP as USB-C for AI, A2A as network layer, and hard-codes MCP stronger / A2A enterprise-niche.
C35.reasoning_bridge: The protocol specs support different scopes, but metaphor and adoption status need attribution and should not be embedded as settled diagram facts.
C35.caveat: Use diagram labels for scope, not market conclusion; attribute or remove the USB-C metaphor.

C36.current_claim_summary: Failure modes derive from A2 x A3 x A4 rather than being a base axis.
C36.reasoning_bridge: Security and system sources support failure modes arising from autonomy, tools, memory, communication, permissions, and topology, so the axis framing is useful but incomplete if it only names A2/A3/A4.

source_support.owasp-llm-top-10-2025: Supports prompt injection, excessive agency, data leakage, and supply-chain style risks.
source_support.agentdojo: Supports indirect prompt injection and tool-use attack evaluation.

C36.caveat: Mention tools, memory, permissions, and product substrate in addition to A2/A3/A4.

C37.current_claim_summary: Cascading errors, context poisoning, hallucination amplification, infinite loops, and agreement bias are agent failure modes.
C37.reasoning_bridge: Security guidance and AgentDojo support prompt injection, untrusted context, tool misuse, and excessive agency; some labels in the page are local taxonomy terms and need cautious wording.

source_support.agentdojo: Supports indirect prompt injection and tool-use attack framing.
source_support.owasp-llm-top-10-2025: Supports prompt injection, excessive agency, data leakage, and supply-chain risks.
source_support.anthropic-writing-tools-for-agents: Supports tool outputs and schemas as part of the reasoning surface.

C37.caveat: Keep local labels, but connect them to recognized security/eval categories and mark agreement bias as weaker terminology.

C38.current_claim_summary: Adding autonomy, shared memory, or A2A communication requires trust boundaries.
C38.reasoning_bridge: OWASP, AgentDojo, Five Eyes, and Operator safety sources directly support treating external content, tool authority, permissions, and delegated actions as trust-boundary problems.

source_support.five-eyes-agentic-ai-guidance: Supports permissions, monitoring, governance, and controls.
source_support.openai-operator-system-card: Supports confirmations/restricted actions for browser-acting agents.
source_support.a2a-protocol-spec: Supports agent-to-agent communication scope where identity/security matter.

C38.caveat: Trust boundaries must include permissions, provenance, policy checks, and audit; HITL alone is not enough.

C39.current_claim_summary: Evaluation is a development loop: start simple, optimize through evals.
C39.reasoning_bridge: Anthropic and OpenAI both support starting with simpler patterns and adding agentic complexity only when evaluation and task needs justify it.

source_support.anthropic-building-effective-agents: Supports simple-first agent design and evaluation-oriented iteration.
source_support.openai-agents-sdk-tracing: Supports trace instrumentation as part of iteration.

C39.caveat: Do not imply that evals are automatic or sufficient without representative local tasks and risk controls.

C40.current_claim_summary: Trace events and metrics decide architecture; evaluation chooses architecture.
C40.reasoning_bridge: Tracing docs and benchmark papers support useful observability and eval dimensions, but architecture should be justified by local task evals, traces, cost, latency, and risk rather than public benchmarks alone.

source_support.openai-agents-sdk-tracing: Supports tracing model calls, tool calls, handoffs, and spans.

C40.caveat: Rewrite as 'local evals and traces justify architecture changes' rather than 'evaluation chooses architecture.'

C41.current_claim_summary: Timeline 2022-2026 covers ReAct, function calls, workflow/agent framing, multi-agent plus CUA, and harness hardening.
C41.reasoning_bridge: The milestone sequence is plausible with available sources, but each year needs its own source and the 2026 wording must be reviewed through 2026-06-03.

source_support.openai-function-calling-2023: Candidate source for the 2023 function-calling milestone; needs catalog entry.

C41.caveat: Use milestone-level annotations and replace stale 'April snapshot' language with 'reviewed through 2026-06-03.'

C42.current_claim_summary: Memory is not big prompt/RAG; the page uses a four-part practical typology.
C42.reasoning_bridge: MemGPT, Generative Agents, and Reflexion support distinct memory mechanisms, while the negative finding limits the four-part split to a practical page taxonomy.

source_support.memgpt: Supports managed memory beyond context window.
source_support.negative-memory-taxonomy: Limits the split from universal ontology to practical typology.

C42.caveat: Say 'a practical split for this page' and avoid implying field-wide standardization.

C44.current_claim_summary: Context window, retrieval, and logs are not memory by default; memory needs read/write policy.
C44.reasoning_bridge: Memory architecture and tool-design sources support explicit control over what is stored, recalled, and promoted into context, while tracing sources keep logs separate from memory.

source_support.opentelemetry-genai-semconv: Supports traces/logs as observability artifacts, not memory by default.

C44.caveat: Define memory operationally by read/write policy, provenance, TTL, and authority rather than storage medium alone.

C46.current_claim_summary: Decision tree maps constraints to minimal sufficient architecture and uses HITL gates for risky errors.
C46.reasoning_bridge: Anthropic/OpenAI support simple-first architecture decisions, while safety sources support HITL as one control for high-risk actions but not a complete mitigation.

source_support.openai-operator-system-card: Supports confirmations/restricted actions for high-risk browser actions.
source_support.negative-hitl-sufficiency: Limits HITL from sufficient control to one part of a control stack.

C46.caveat: Pair HITL with scoped permissions, trusted UI/context, audit trails, policy checks, monitoring, and untrusted-content boundaries.

C52.current_claim_summary: Strong emergence is usually an excuse for unpredictability and rarely provable in engineering systems.
C52.reasoning_bridge: The research backlog found stronger support for mechanism-first explanations through topology, memory, tools, coordination, and traces than for broad philosophical claims about emergence.

source_support.negative-emergence: Supports moving emergence language to cautious, mechanism-first framing.
source_support.generative-agents: Supports architecture-specific explanation of social-agent behavior.

C52.caveat: Frame as an engineering preference: explain behavior through mechanisms and traces before invoking emergence.

C53.current_claim_summary: 2026 signals do not change the taxonomy; they strengthen execution substrate/A6 and failure/eval sections.
C53.reasoning_bridge: OpenAI docs, A2A v1.0.0, OpenTelemetry, and Five Eyes guidance support updated substrate/protocol/safety/eval evidence, but they do not prove the taxonomy is complete or frozen.

source_support.openai-agents-sdk-docs: Supports current agent execution substrate vocabulary.

C53.caveat: Date-stamp as reviewed 2026-06-03 and avoid saying future developments cannot change the map.

C54.current_claim_summary: The page ends with what remains from the original and a list of what has been added.
C54.reasoning_bridge: Internal task files can support process history, but process history is not public evidence for the taxonomy and should move out of the main flow.

source_support.tasks-readme: Supports internal rewrite process history only.
source_support.audit-annotation-ui-spec: Supports moving process notes away from public claim annotations.

C54.caveat: Move to appendix/hidden notes or delete from the main read; do not annotate as taxonomy evidence.

C55.current_claim_summary: The footer is a global source list without links, claim mapping, access dates, or evidence strength.
C55.reasoning_bridge: The local source catalog and annotation spec directly show that source existence must be separated from claim support and time-sensitive docs need access dates.

source_support.research-sources-catalog: Provides reviewed source records and source limitations.
source_support.audit-annotation-ui-spec: Defines claim-level registry and annotation requirements.
source_support.negative-product-architecture: Shows why footer-only product docs are not enough.

C55.caveat: Replace footer-only bibliography with claim-level annotations and a source drawer/registry; keep public UI visually light.

recommended_claim_markup.marker_accessibility: Use a button with aria-label; evidence strength must not be conveyed by color alone.

public_marker_rules.avoid: Square bracket citation markers in the main reading flow.

drawer_fields: what each source does not support
